description_long: This is a black, circular-bladed skimmer or strainer shovel with a long, straight handle. The blade is flat and perforated with multiple evenly spaced round holes, allowing for liquid drainage while retaining solids. The handle is ribbed for grip and ends in a small circular hole, likely for hanging or storage. The entire object appears to be made from a single piece of molded plastic, giving it a seamless and lightweight appearance. The surface is smooth and slightly glossy, reflecting light in some areas. There are no visible brand markings, decorations, or signs of wear.
description: A black, perforated skimmer shovel with a round flat blade and a long ribbed handle, made of smooth plastic.
description_view_0: Front view showing the round, perforated blade and the full length of the ribbed handle with a hanging hole at the end.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin, flat profile of the blade and handle.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the perforated blade and handle details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thinness and flatness of the object.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, ribbed plastic handle with a circular hanging hole at the end.
attribute_blade_type: Flat, round blade with multiple circular perforations; no edge curvature or reinforcement.
attribute_mounting_location: Circular hole at the end of the handle for hanging.
